cards.title: Legal Rights & Values
cards.content:
• Nomination (Section 39) vs. Assignment (Section 38)
• Absolute vs. Conditional Assignment
• Surrender Value and Paid-Up Value
• Policy Loans and Foreclosure

cards.title: Key Points
cards.content: Nominee is only a receiver/trustee, NOT the owner. Can be changed anytime. If nominee is minor, an 'Appointee' must be named.

cards.title: 1. Absolute Assignment
cards.content: Transfer of complete ownership. CRITICAL: This automatically CANCELS existing nomination.

cards.title: 2. Conditional Assignment
cards.content: Subject to conditions (e.g., Bank Loan). Policy returns to owner once condition is met (loan repaid).

cards.title: 3. Surrender vs Paid-Up
cards.content:
Surrender: Terminating for cash. 
Paid-Up: Continuing with reduced Sum Assured when premiums stop after 2-3 years.

cards.title: 4. Loans & Foreclosure
cards.content: Can take loan up to 90% of Surrender Value. Foreclosure occurs if (Loan + Interest) > Surrender Value.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: Appointee needed: When nominee is a minor.
